figures.caption: Figure 1: Trajectory snapshots on the eight Gaussian targets with sampling step K = 200. Top row, deterministic flow. The empirical distribution is closest to ρ∗ at k = 50, and degenerates into a sum of Dirac masses on the eight critical points of U∗ θ as k increases, in agreement with Theorem 2. The deterministic stopping rule in (16) predicts k∗ = 49, marked by the proximity of the k = 50 panel to the data. Bottom row, Langevin SDE. The empirical distribution converges to the Gibbs distribution ρ∗θ by k ≈ 100 and remains visually unchanged at k = 200. Additional integration past τ∗Lang does not degrade the bound.
